title: Install
text:
When deploying a node in a new zone, ensure that the zone has properly configured WAN access. Wait for it to appear in [perfsonar](https://perfsonar.nrp-nautilus.io/maddash-webui/index.cgi?dashboard=Nautilus%20Mesh) and wait for the corresponding row and column in every dashboard to turn green for this zone.

It might take up to 6 hours for the node to appear and be fully connected to the mesh.

### New node in existing zone

Test the new node storage access. Run the storage test task from the [ansible playbook](https://gitlab.nrp-nautilus.io/prp/nautilus-ansible).

```bash
./run.sh storage-test <new_node>
```

All tests should succeed.

### Manually checking the node connectivity

Using the `pstest` and `pstesth` commands from [ansible zsh script](https://gitlab.nrp-nautilus.io/prp/nautilus-ansible/-/blob/master/zsh-scripts.sh?ref_type=heads#L52), login to the perfsonar pod on the new node and some other node you know is working properly. (pstesth is for host network, pstest for overlay. Both should work fine.)

Run the iperf3 test between the two nodes.

```
iperf3 -s
```

```
ieprf3 -c <ip_of_node_above>
```

The throughput should be close to the node's max NIC bandwidth. Having it in megabits range usually means having a firewall on the path. Having no data passed means MTU issues or other network issues.
